1.content:
Walter Freeman had always loved traveling and being in the outdoors. His job required long hours and with a family to provide for, did not get to travel as much as he wanted to. However, this summer he had planned a year in advance a vacation get away to Tahoe at his father’s beautiful timeshare cabin with company to come along with him.

Walter lived in Flagstaff Arizona and leaving on a Saturday morning meant leaving at 6 am to arrive near 8 at night. He and the people accompanying him packed their bags and headed off. At around 7:30 p.m. they had saw the wide open views and the mountains were vast. “Be very careful up these mountains,” someone had told him in his car.

Walter wasn’t used to these roads. They were elevated on the mountains so veering off would have seriously injured anyone or even death. “I got this, I’m the best driver around,” Walter jokingly says while feeling uneasy.

Walter takes a sip of water to ease his nerves but as he turned a corner, another car was speeding towards your side of the road, and crashes into your car. Everything happened so fast and so sudden, and the very thing Walter feared ended up happening. The two cars were so close to edge of the mountain that the collision veered them off and tumbled down the mountain. Screams were released as you and the other car flipped for what felt like hours until the cars were now landed in the vast forest of trees.

Everything was white from Walter’s perspective and the last thing he remembered was the sheer agony of falling off a cliff.

Suddenly, Walter opens his eyes slowly to hear medical terms and equipment clanking near him.

You hear a voice say, “Good good, he is awake.”
Walter opens his eyes to see a doctor and his assistant staring down at your face in an amazed look.

“Walter, Walter,” the doctor explains, getting your attention. “I am Doctor Grant. I am a brain surgeon and we just want to make sure you are ok.”

Immediately, the word brain surgeon had scared Walter half to death as his heart sank into his chest. “Brain surgeon? Why? Why? Am I ok?” Walter questions in a now panicked state.

“It’s alright Walter, it’s ok. I know you have many questions,” Dr. Grant explained. Dr. Grant was a chubby Caucasian man with glasses who looked in his 60’s and his assistant was Albert Chang, a skinny Japanese-American middle aged man.

“I need you to just understand that we tried everything we can for you and the others who traveled with you on your trip,” Dr. Grant explained, “However, it was a serious fall and not everyone who left on the trip is the same as they were when the fall happened.”

“What do you mean by that? Am I ok? Are they ok?” Walter asked in a concerned but frightened tone.

“Well we had to perform a transplant,” Dr. Grant explained. “See we take a brain out of the body and we put it into a body that is healthy and better than the body that had been otherwise damaged beyond comprehension.”

“Well whose brain had to be removed,” Walter asked sarcastically, almost believing it to be bullshit.

Dr. Grant took off his glasses and told Walter that
1.title: Turn for the Worst

11.content:
“Me? How is the possible?” Walter questioned, now panicked.

“Your body was damaged beyond repair. The only thing we could have done was put your brain inside of someone who body showed little to no damage from the crash,” Dr. Grant explained.

Walter, now in tears, was distraught but curious to know why body he was in. “Whose body am I in now.”

We successfully transferred your brain to the body of
11.title: You had received the transplant

choices: I suppose we go on to the house
I’m not going anywhere until they give me more answers.
111.content:
Walter’s heart sank as he tilted his forward forward to reveal his toned body complimented with his wife’s beautiful breasts he always loved to feel that were now his.

Walter had traveled with his wife and kids to Tahoe, hoping to have a fun vacation. Being packed into his wife’s body was the last thing he expected. “Your wife had a serious concussion, serious enough to destroy her brain,” Dr. Grant explains in a sensitive manner. “Your body was just too damaged from large shards of glass pierced in your body.”

“That means my wife is?” Walter questions but knowing the answer. “Gone, yes. It’s a lot to take in, but you are so fortunate to be alive Mr...or should I say Ms. Freeman,” Dr. Grant says optimistically.

Walter moves his perfectly pedicured feet and presumed to rise from his bed.

“Uh uh,” Dr. Grant warns you. “We really should take it slow, you’re still in shock.” That didn’t stop Walter, now his gorgeous wife, from entering the waiting to room to find the rest of his family there.

“My kids!” Walter exclaims as he embraces them. James and Danica were crying as they embraced their father...now mother. “We miss mommy so much, I can’t believe she’s dead,” cried Danica.
“Does that mean your out mother and father?” James questioned with a confused but tragic face.

“I don’t know kids,” your feminine voice said out loud. Walter hearing his wife’s voice, the voice that seduced him on their first date now belonged to him. A year runs down his face as the situation fully syncs in.

“What do we do now?” both your kids ask you.
You reply
111.title: Your wife, Camille

choices: Both of his parents Dennis and Dorothy were  there to greet them
Camille’s mother Penny was there to greet them
1111.content:
Walter talks to Dr. Grant about coming to terms with his new fantastic body. He suggested to make frequent visits for some check ups but sends Walter home.

On the way home, James and Danica whisper about their new father’s well being. With Walter driving home he looks at the front seat to see it empty and begins to cry, realizing your wife is truly gone and that you are both father and mother now...quite literally.

“Dad, it’s going to be ok,” James says as he puts his hand on his shoulder from the back seat.
“Thank you sweetly,” Walter says. “I just want to be a good single parent, but I miss your mother so much.” A moment of silence sets in as the kids begin to shed a few tears and talk about the memories they had of their mother.

They had approached their home, finally. Walter opens the door to find that
1111.title: I suppose we go on to the house

choices: He accepts his new life as Camille
11111.content:
Your parents at first staring at son, now turned into their daughter-in-law then start to cry. They crowd around your family as they begin to hug Walter.

“We are so sorry this happened to you Walter,” Dorothy whispers into Walter’s ear with tears streaming down her face.

“Yeah, those times you wouldn’t want to help me on the farm and I called you sissy, I didn’t think you’d turn into sissy,” Dennis jokingly says, trying to alleviate the tension. “Dennis!” Dorothy remarks. “It’s ok Mom,I could use a little laughter right now,” Walter says.

Walter heads up to his room and shuts the door as he has no clue what’s next in life.
11111.title: Both of his parents Dennis and Dorothy were  th...

114.content: Someone else in the family
114.title: Someone else in your family

choices: A 21 year old sorority girl named Jessica
A single father of 2 children named Michael
115.content: Someone involved in the crash
115.title: Someone else

12.content:
The news that Walter’s love of his life was now in someone else’s body was too much for him to handle. The one he had married and have kids with was now in someone else’ body.

“Where is my wife now?” Walter asked, understandably shaken up. “Well Walter, we want you to know that this was the best option, otherwise your wife could be dead,” Dr. Grant explained. “I can take it,” Walter said confidently. Dr. Grant looked down and told Walter that your wife’s brain was now in the body of
12.title: Your wife’s, Camille

choices: To leave Camille behind, as Walter cannot accept the situation
122.content:
Walter’s heart sank, now aware that his best friend and boss, Greg was gone. Not only that but Camille was now in control of his body. Walter had gone on a trip with his wife, Greg, and his wife and now could only think about how Greg’s wife would take this news.

“Can I see Greg..I mean my wife?” Walter said in a confused tone. “Well since the surgery was a success, Camille is in the other room, and you can see her,” Dr. Grant said. Walter got up slowly to approach his wife, now in the body of his buddy Greg. The tears started rolling down his face, attempting to imagine that your wife was still there, but was too fixated on the male on the bed. Then suddenly Camille looked at Walter and whispered, “Hello..honey. What do you think of my new look?”

“Darling, I don’t know what to say, are you really alright being in the body of my closest friend.” Walter questioned, while attempting to be understanding of the situation.

“Walter,” Camille began to explain, “as you know, there is no other choice. My body was greatly damaged and there was nothing that I could do. The other option could have been to die and that’s all I can accept as my reality now.”

The acceptance of the situation was shocking but started to make sense for Walter as he was happy that she was not dead. But still, he lost a friend in the process still. Dr. Grant stood there and told them that they were free to go back to their homes and continue to live life as normal as possible. So Walter and Camille decided to
122.title: Your boss, Greg Smith

1221.content:
After an awkward car ride with Walter as he watched Camille checking out his new body, they arrived home to their children. As they approached he house, James and Danica along with their babysitter were shocked to see Greg, who was like an uncle to them, come out of the car to embrace them.

“Kids, I missed you guys terribly,” Camille said with tears rolling down her face. The kids were still taken back, having been informed by their babysitter about the whole situation. Walter, watching the whole interaction knew that hints would probably not be the same again.

As they all walked into the house, Walter knew that they needed to talk about how to deal with everything.
“Honey,” Walter said feeling odd for just calling a man Honey, “I think we do need to talk about how we go forward from here on out.”

The two go upstairs, shit the door, and begin to talk to each other about how to navigate this.
1221.title: Go back home

15.content: Someone else from your family had received the transplant.
15.title: Someone else

155.content: Some body else you knew
155.title: Someone else you knew

Two sisters with both their husbands
1553.content:
“Unfortunately, I have bad news,” Dr. Grant paused.

“Just tell me!” Walter demanded.

Dr. Grant said with his head pointed down, “your whole family had to have their brains put into different bodies, including you.”

The gravity of the situation had gotten far worse than Walter had anticipated. Walter asked who the new hosts were. Dr. grant explained that it was
1553.title: It’s more complicated than that

info.brief_description: A trip to Tahoe goes awry but a transplant may save some lives
info.description:
A beautiful Saturday morning seemed like just a normal and perfect day to take the most important people in your life on a road trip to Tahoe. You are 40 year old Walter Freeman and this is your vacation that you have planned for a year when suddenly the treacherous turns up the mountains caused your car to collide with another. Both your cars tumble down the mountain and both parties fates are in the brilliant hands of one of the greatest brain surgeons in the world.

Characters:

Walter Freeman: 40 year old father of James and Danica Freeman. Husband to Camille Freeman. Walter is a middle aged man with a beer belly, balding throughout his head, and going through a mid life crisis. Usually preferring the outdoors, Walter’s job deprives him of that passion, until his vacation to Tahoe.

Camille Freeman: 38 year old mother to James and Danica Freeman. Wife of Walter Freeman. Blonde with blue eyes and an hourglass figure, she tries to maintain her weight in their gym at home. She is a stay at home mom who is always cleaning after her messy children but loves her family very much.

James Freeman: 16 year old teen of Walter and Camille Freeman. Skinny kid with glasses who hunches over a lot.When he’s not at school, James is glued to the television, playing video games on a daily basis, making his grades drop.

Danica Freeman: 12 year old child of Walter and Camille Freeman. Blonde hair with blue eyes. Tries to stay I shape like her momma. A girly girl and Tik Tok influencer, she is always on her phone thinking of the next Tik Tok she can make for her 488,000 followers.

Dorothy Freeman: 67 year old mother of Walter Freeman and grandmother to James and Danica. Brunette/Gray hair. Slightly chubby with poor eyesight without her thick glasses. Dorothy lives with her sons family along with her husband Dennis Freeman.

Dennis Freeman: 70 year old father of Walter Freeman and grandfather or James and Danica. Liking the outdoors like his son, he is unable to explore like he used to and needs a cane from having ankle problems. Him and his wife Dorothy live with his sons family.

Penny Sapin: Camille’s 60 year old mother and grandmother to James and Danica. Although 60, she stays in shape like her daughter and granddaughter. Blonde hair, but turning slightly gray with a great attitude on life. Husband died 5 years earlier, but still is open for relationships.

Greg Smith: Walter’s boss and best friend since high school, him and Walter try to hangout whenever they can outside the office. Call it favoritism, but Walter is Greg’s #1 guy at their law firm. 48 years old, but fit with glasses and a go get em attitude.

Debbie Smith: Greg’s wife and Camille’s closest friend. Debbie is a housewife who loves to sell things on EBay all the time. Debbie is a 46 year old woman, red hair, and beautiful with an hourglass figure. Her and Camille work out as much as they can together.

There are more characters that will pop up throughout the story.
